summary: See clues file for full details. 11 tossups, 31 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 11 tossups and 31 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: This artist depicted the donor offering the altarpiece itself to St.
examples: This artist depicted the donor offering the altarpiece itself to St.

clues.clue: This artist's best-known work includes grisaille depictions of the seven virtues and vices, which run parallel to three fresco cycles depicting the lives of Christ, the Virgin, and Joachim.
examples: This artist's best-known work includes grisaille depictions of the seven virtues and vices, which run parallel to three fresco cycles depicting the lives of Christ, the Virgin, and Joachim.

clues.clue: A building with a cobalt-blue ceiling houses a painting by this artist which depicts ten small (*) angels crying out in pain while darting around the title scene, his Lamentation.
examples: A building with a cobalt-blue ceiling houses a painting by this artist which depicts ten small (*) angels crying out in pain while darting around the title scene, his Lamentation.

clues.clue: This student of Cimabue received his most famous commission from the penitent usurer Enrico Scrovegni.
examples: This student of Cimabue received his most famous commission from the penitent usurer Enrico Scrovegni.

clues.clue: Although this artist was once credited with painting the Coronation altarpiece for the Baroncelli Chapel, most scholars now ascribe the altarpiece to his godson, Taddeo Gaddi.
examples: Although this artist was once credited with painting the Coronation altarpiece for the Baroncelli Chapel, most scholars now ascribe the altarpiece to his godson, Taddeo Gaddi.

clues.clue: For one commission, this artist lined the lower register of a chapel with monochrome depictions of seven virtues and vices.
examples: For one commission, this artist lined the lower register of a chapel with monochrome depictions of seven virtues and vices.

clues.clue: Angels despair above an inclined rock with a bare tree on top of it in this artist's naturalistic depiction of the Lamentation.
examples: Angels despair above an inclined rock with a bare tree on top of it in this artist's naturalistic depiction of the Lamentation.

clues.clue: This artist trained in the workshop of the (*) Byzantine-influenced master Cimabue .
examples: This artist trained in the workshop of the (*) Byzantine-influenced master Cimabue .

clues.clue: This artist included a deep blue ceiling fresco in his paintings for a building originally attached to the palace of Enrico Scrovegni.
examples: This artist included a deep blue ceiling fresco in his paintings for a building originally attached to the palace of Enrico Scrovegni.

clues.clue: Franco Sacchetti recounts how after being commissioned to paint a shield with a coat of arms, this artist instead painted it "armed to the teeth." This artist depicted a donor holding the very triptych he was depicted in in one of the earliest examples of the Droste effect.
examples: Franco Sacchetti recounts how after being commissioned to paint a shield with a coat of arms, this artist instead painted it "armed to the teeth." This artist depicted a donor holding the very triptych he was depicted in in one of the earliest examples of the Droste effect.

clues.clue: By showing expressions in moments of action in paintings such as Saint Francis Receiving the Stigmata, this artist broke from the Byzantine style.
examples: By showing expressions in moments of action in paintings such as Saint Francis Receiving the Stigmata, this artist broke from the Byzantine style.
